intro: Astraea digitata, also known as the Starfruit or Astraea, is a succulent plant species native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive star-shaped leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Astraea digitata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
